free_text: Excitation function for the reaction
13C(alpha,ngamma)16O
ONR tandem accelerator.
Currents were generally of the order of 0.1 uA.
The energy spread of the beam was about two or three
keV.
Gamma rays were detected using a 10.2 cm x
10.2 cm NaI(Tl) crystal mounted on a 12.7 cm CBS 7819
photomultiplier.
The 13C target was prepared by radio-frequency heating
of a disc of 0.045 cm tantalum in an atmosphere of
methyl iodide in which the carbon was enriched to 54.0%
by weight of 13C. The target thickness was determined
using the narrow resonance in the 13C(p,g)14N reaction
at 448 keV. The result obtained for the thickness was
13.5 ug/cm2, accurate to about 5%; this corresponds
to approximately 10 keV for 5.5 MeV alphas and 7 keV
for 9.4 MeV alphas.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
No explanation of relative units is given.
An excitation function was obtained by summing, for
each spectrum, the number of pulses in channels
corresponding to energies from 4.98 to 7.79 MeV;
this "window" includes 6.13, 6.92 and 7.12 MeV
radiation.
No information about source of uncertainties
Fig.3 from Nucl.Phys.41(1963)353
SD: SF7=G added to REACTION code.
E-LVL -> E-MIN/MAX (values were changed). Accuracy of
digitized data presentation revised.
Corrections have been applied for dead-time losses and
gamma-ray absorption in the target backing.
Probable error in the absolute cross
section is less than 20%.
Statistical error from the graphic bars
Fig.4 from Nucl.Phys.41(1963)353
SD: SF7=G added to REACTION code. Accuracy
of digitized data presentation revised. E-LVL -> E.
ERR-SYS -> ERR-T.
No information about source of uncertainties
Tbl.1 from Nucl.Phys.41(1963)353
SD: Data presentation revised. STATUS
updated. FLAG deleted (info moved to DATA-MN-CM/MX-CM.
